prompt: Say: This is my brother and that is my cousin.
correctAnswer: Este es mi hermano y ese es mi primo.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: My friends are very kind and funny.
correctAnswer: Mis amigos son muy amables y divertidos.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: Say: My sister lives near my parents.
correctAnswer: Mi hermana vive cerca de mis padres.
hints: Practise this phrase out loud.

prompt: a grandmother
correctAnswer: una abuela
hints: Your mother's or father's mother.

prompt: Say: I have two brothers and one sister.
correctAnswer: Tengo dos hermanos y una hermana.
hints: 'Tener' means 'to have'.

prompt: a neighbour
correctAnswer: un vecino
hints: Someone who lives near you.

prompt: Say: My mother is a doctor.
correctAnswer: Mi madre es médica.
hints: Use 'ser' for professions.

prompt: Say: I live with my family.
correctAnswer: Vivo con mi familia.
hints: Short, simple sentence about home life.

prompt: a best friend
correctAnswer: un mejor amigo
hints: Your closest friend.

prompt: Say: He is my uncle.
correctAnswer: Él es mi tío.
hints: Introduce a family member.

prompt: Ask: Are you visiting your family this weekend?
correctAnswer: ¿Vas a visitar a tu familia este fin de semana?
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Say: We usually have dinner together on Sundays.
correctAnswer: Normalmente cenamos juntos los domingos.
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Ask: Would you like to come over for dinner on Friday?
correctAnswer: ¿Te gustaría venir a cenar el viernes?
hints: Pay attention to the sentence structure.

prompt: Say: My parents got married twenty years ago.
correctAnswer: Mis padres se casaron hace veinte años.
hints: Use the preterite of reflexive 'casarse' + 'hace' + time.

prompt: Say: My cousin is younger than me.
correctAnswer: Mi primo es más joven que yo.
hints: Use 'más... que' for comparisons.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Me _____ mucho pasar tiempo con mis amigos. (gusta)
correctAnswer: gusta
hints: 'Gustar' agrees with the thing liked, not the person.

prompt: Say: We celebrated my grandmother's birthday last week.
correctAnswer: Celebramos el cumpleaños de mi abuela la semana pasada.
hints: Use the preterite for a completed past event.

prompt: Say: My older brother helps me with my homework.
correctAnswer: Mi hermano mayor me ayuda con los deberes.
hints: Use present tense; 'mayor' means older.

prompt: Ask: How many people are in your family?
correctAnswer: ¿Cuántas personas hay en tu familia?
hints: 'Cuántas' for a countable feminine noun.

prompt: Say: We get together every Christmas.
correctAnswer: Nos reunimos todas las Navidades.
hints: Use the reflexive 'reunirse' for getting together.

prompt: Say: I grew up with my grandparents in a small town.
correctAnswer: Crecí con mis abuelos en un pueblo pequeño.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Say: My best friend always gives me honest advice.
correctAnswer: Mi mejor amigo siempre me da consejos sinceros.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Say: We stay close even though we live in different cities.
correctAnswer: Seguimos unidos aunque vivimos en ciudades diferentes.
hints: Notice the tense or modal used.

prompt: Say: My parents want me to call them every week.
correctAnswer: Mis padres quieren que los llame cada semana.
hints: After 'querer que', use the present subjunctive.

prompt: Say: I hope my friends can come to my party.
correctAnswer: Espero que mis amigos puedan venir a mi fiesta.
hints: 'Esperar que' triggers the subjunctive.

prompt: Say: I have known my best friend since primary school.
correctAnswer: Conozco a mi mejor amigo desde la escuela primaria.
hints: Use present tense + 'desde' for an ongoing relationship.

prompt: Choose the best response. What does your family mean to you?
correctAnswer: Mi familia es mi mayor apoyo, sin ella no sería lo que soy.
hints: Use 'sin' + conditional for a hypothetical.

prompt: Say: My aunt moved abroad two years ago and we miss her a lot.
correctAnswer: Mi tía se mudó al extranjero hace dos años y la echamos mucho de menos.
hints: Preterite for the move; 'echar de menos' = to miss.

prompt: Say: It is good that you spend time with your children.
correctAnswer: Es bueno que pases tiempo con tus hijos.
hints: After 'es bueno que', use the present subjunctive.

prompt: Say: My siblings and I used to argue a lot when we were children.
correctAnswer: Mis hermanos y yo nos peleábamos mucho cuando éramos niños.
hints: Use the imperfect for repeated past actions.

prompt: Say: Even when we disagree, we try to listen respectfully.
correctAnswer: Incluso cuando no estamos de acuerdo, intentamos escuchar con respeto.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: Say: Family traditions help us stay connected across generations.
correctAnswer: Las tradiciones familiares nos ayudan a mantenernos unidos entre generaciones.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: Say: Good friendships require time, trust, and patience.
correctAnswer: Las buenas amistades requieren tiempo, confianza y paciencia.
hints: Focus on natural phrasing and word order.

prompt: Say: I wish my parents had been more open-minded when I was a teenager.
correctAnswer: Ojalá mis padres hubieran sido más abiertos de mente cuando era adolescente.
hints: 'Ojalá' + past perfect subjunctive expresses a regret about the past.

prompt: Say: The bond between siblings is shaped by both shared experiences and individual differences.
correctAnswer: El vínculo entre hermanos está moldeado tanto por las experiencias compartidas como por las diferencias individuales.
hints: Use 'estar' + past participle for a resulting state; 'tanto... como' for both... and.

prompt: Say: Maintaining long-distance friendships demands consistent effort from both sides.
correctAnswer: Mantener amistades a distancia exige un esfuerzo constante de ambas partes.
hints: Gerund as subject; 'exigir' = to demand.

prompt: Say: However much families may argue, they usually support each other in a crisis.
correctAnswer: Por mucho que discutan las familias, normalmente se apoyan mutuamente en una crisis.
hints: 'Por mucho que' + subjunctive expresses concession.

prompt: Say: My relationship with my parents has evolved considerably since I moved out.
correctAnswer: Mi relación con mis padres ha evolucionado considerablemente desde que me fui de casa.
hints: Present perfect for change up to now; 'desde que' + preterite.
